title: Internet banking security event monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: HKMA TM-G-2 §3 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HKMA TM-G-2 §3 (Governance of technology risk) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.30.11: Internet banking security event monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.30.11' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HKMA TM-G-2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes web and sourcetypes ib:access. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain `apac_material_services.csv` for important business services and regulator tags. (2) Send weekly digests to CRO and technology risk committees. (3) Validate DB Connect concurrency for audit peak windows. (4) Archive results to a restricted summary index for retention governance. Indexes required: `index=web`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ib:access`.
visualization: Column chart (events by regulator), Table (privileged actions), Timeline (notification milestones). Key fields: `geo`, `uri`, `user`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: TRM evidence during scheduled audits, controlled remediation cycles, or migration windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Banking system audit trails, cloud infrastructure logs, vulnerability scan outputs, privileged access management logs, change management records, business continuity evidence, incident response logs, outsourcing management records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `apac_material_services.csv` for important business services and regulator tags. (2) Send weekly digests to CRO and technology risk committees. (3) Validate DB Connect concurrency for audit peak windows. (4) Archive results to a restricted summary index for retention governance.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=web sourcetype="ib:access" earliest=-24h
| stats count by uri, user, geo
| search uri="*/ib/*" AND geo!="HK"
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Internet banking security event monitoring (HKMA TM-G-2)** — Supports HKMA TM-G-2 supervisory examination readiness for internet banking security event monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: Banking system audit trails, cloud infrastructure logs, vulnerability scan outputs, privileged access management logs, change management records, business continuity evidence, incident response logs, outsourcing management records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: web; **sourcetype**: ib:access. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=web, sourcetype="ib:access", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by uri, user, geo** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Internet banking security event monitoring (HKMA TM-G-2)** — Supports HKMA TM-G-2 supervisory examination readiness for internet banking security event monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: Banking system audit trails, cloud infrastructure logs, vulnerability scan outputs, privileged access management logs, change management records, business continuity evidence, incident response logs, outsourcing management records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with regional reporting cutoffs in your runbook. Document any expected regulatory reporting window or approved exception before escalating.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Column chart (events by regulator), Table (privileged actions), Timeline (notification milestones).
grandmaExplanation: We help you show technology risk management and financial-sector technology evidence your regulator expects, with dated activity you can hand over.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model).
